text: RAM budget is unmeasured speculation; overlap of ollama model residency + Claude SDK subprocess is the real risk
Fallback collapses 'host unreachable' and 'model inadequate' into same escape hatch, erasing experiment signal
Background polling laptop every 60s is noise; lazy resolution with negative caching is better
Journal analysis proves plumbing, not portability; too easy for the Seidr experiment bar
Context injection via loadContextForOllama() makes Hugin responsible for semantic context policy — ad-hoc Seidr risk
Non-streaming breaks Hugin's existing operational contract (incremental logs, partial capture on timeout)
Adding a third bespoke branch in pollOnce() creates maintenance burden; should use clean executor interface
'Harder task within first week' is too vague — name the task before implementation, it shapes what metadata the runtime must preserve
Context-refs needs a precise contract: grammar, serialization order, missing-ref handling, truncation logging
Byte budget is wrong abstraction; need deterministic character budget with explicit truncation rules
RAM measurement needs an admission rule at runtime, not just a pre-implementation gate
Journal needs host_requested vs host_effective, model_requested vs model_effective, context refs resolved, truncation status
Laptop support risks mixing model/host/network variables in first experiment; should be controlled
